as you've thrown a new you know a card into the game you know and it's i think very uh... very much related to waiting is uh... it's easy to look at the cost the potential downside cost of action but people very seldom look at the company definite cost of inaction and there's an expression in in fighting i train with a lot of uh... professional fighters in u.s.c. and so forth and others there's an expression borrowed from boxing which is the lot in u.s.c. training which is everyone has a plan until they get hit in the face and the point being that you can theorize and work on speed bag and work on your job on the mix and everything else and and just wait and wait and plan and plan to prepare to parent and get the ring and you have this perfect you know twenty seven point strategy and then some like punches you right in the face and it is all vanishes and i think it's important to recognize that the rule rather than the exception and that the the important point is getting the ball into
